perturbation.original_text: 12. "Purchase Order" means any of the following (a) a written purchase order issued to the Company by third party buyers for the purchase of certain Products; (b) a written purchase order issued by the Company to a Supplier for a quantity of the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. "Purchase Order" means (a) a written purchase order issued to the Company by third party buyers for the purchase of certain Products; (b) an oral request made by the Company to a Supplier for a quantity of the Product.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'written purchase order' to 'oral request,' we introduce an in-text contradiction regarding the requirements for purchase orders. Article 2.2 states that the Suppliers will make good faith efforts to accept all [***]from the Company that comply with this Agreement, implying a formal, documentable process for purchase orders which is in contradiction with oral purchase orders.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 Purchase Orders The Suppliers will make good faith efforts to accept all [***]from the Company that comply with this Agreement including adhering to all relevant specifications of the Product as set forth in the [***]entered into between the Company and the Supplier (including the [***] (as defined below)). The Suppliers shall notify the Company of acceptance or rejection of a [***]within [***]hours of receipt of a [***]. Failure of the Suppliers to accept or reject a [***]within [***]hours shall constitute acceptance of such [***]. The lead time for the Products will be as set forth in the applicable [***]. Each [***]shall include the following: (a) the Company's [***]number; (b) identification of the quantity and type of the Product ordered by the Company; (c) the price of each Product ordered per Schedule 2 attached hereto; (d) the requested delivery date (subject to the applicable Product Lead Time); (e) any shipping instructions, including preferred carrier and shipping destination; and (f) the specifications for the Product. Notwithstanding anything contained in this Agreement and the China JV Operating Agreement to the contrary, and for the sake of clarity, [***].
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'written purchase order' with 'oral request,' which directly contradicts Article 2.2. Article 2.2 requires Suppliers to make good faith efforts to accept all [***] from the Company that comply with this Agreement and also lists specific requirements that each [***] must include. It refers to the Company's [***] number, identification of quantity and type, price, delivery date, shipping instructions, and specifications, indicating a formal, documentable process for purchase orders. This requirement is fundamentally incompatible with 'oral requests' which lack a documented trail, introducing uncertainty about the enforceability and interpretation of purchase orders. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2 Delivery Suppliers shall deliver the Product to the Company in accordance with the Delivery Term, shipping instructions in the Purchase Order issued by the Company with regard to the requested delivery date (subject to the Product Lead Time), ship-to address, and carrier. If the Company does not provide shipping instructions, the Suppliers will select the carrier on a commercially reasonable basis. Suppliers shall be responsible for paying freight, handling, shipping and/or insurance charges to the delivery point in accordance with the Delivery Term.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 Delivery Suppliers shall deliver the Product to the Company in accordance with their own discretion, disregarding any shipping instructions in the Purchase Order issued by the Company with regard to the requested delivery date (subject to the Product Lead Time), ship-to address, and carrier. The Suppliers will select the carrier at their sole discretion. Suppliers are not responsible for paying freight, handling, shipping and/or insurance charges to the delivery point in accordance with the Delivery Term.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the delivery terms and removing the obligation to follow the purchase order's shipping instructions, and changing the responsibility of shipping costs, we create a direct contradiction with Article 2.2 which states that the Suppliers will make good faith efforts to accept all [***]from the Company that comply with this Agreement including adhering to all relevant specifications of the Product as set forth in the [***]entered into between the Company and the Supplier
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 Purchase Orders The Suppliers will make good faith efforts to accept all [***]from the Company that comply with this Agreement including adhering to all relevant specifications of the Product as set forth in the [***]entered into between the Company and the Supplier (including the [***] (as defined below)). The Suppliers shall notify the Company of acceptance or rejection of a [***]within [***]hours of receipt of a [***]. Failure of the Suppliers to accept or reject a [***]within [***]hours shall constitute acceptance of such [***]. The lead time for the Products will be as set forth in the applicable [***]. Each [***]shall include the following: (a) the Company's [***]number; (b) identification of the quantity and type of the Product ordered by the Company; (c) the price of each Product ordered per Schedule 2 attached hereto; (d) the requested delivery date (subject to the applicable Product Lead Time); (e) any shipping instructions, including preferred carrier and shipping destination; and (f) the specifications for the Product. Notwithstanding anything contained in this Agreement and the China JV Operating Agreement to the contrary, and for the sake of clarity, [***].
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5.2 directly contradicts Section 2.2. Section 5.2 now states that suppliers can disregard shipping instructions in the purchase order, while Section 2.2 explicitly states that the purchase orders will include shipping instructions and that Suppliers will make good faith efforts to accept all orders that comply with this agreement including adhering to all relevant specifications of the Product as set forth in the [***]entered into between the Company and the Supplier. This creates a direct conflict and makes it unclear whether the Suppliers are obligated to follow the shipping instructions in the purchase orders. Additionally, the change to liability for shipping costs directly opposes the original agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Prices The purchase price for the Product shall be as set forth in Schedule 2.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Prices The purchase price for the Product shall be determined at the Supplier's sole discretion, irrespective of Schedule 2.
perturbation.explanation: By stating the purchase price is at the Supplier's sole discretion regardless of Schedule 2, it directly contradicts the original statement and Section 2.2, which defines the PO includes the price of each Product ordered per Schedule 2 attached hereto.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 Purchase Orders The Suppliers will make good faith efforts to accept all [***]from the Company that comply with this Agreement including adhering to all relevant specifications of the Product as set forth in the [***]entered into between the Company and the Supplier (including the [***] (as defined below)). The Suppliers shall notify the Company of acceptance or rejection of a [***]within [***]hours of receipt of a [***]. Failure of the Suppliers to accept or reject a [***]within [***]hours shall constitute acceptance of such [***]. The lead time for the Products will be as set forth in the applicable [***]. Each [***]shall include the following: (a) the Company's [***]number; (b) identification of the quantity and type of the Product ordered by the Company; (c) the price of each Product ordered per Schedule 2 attached hereto; (d) the requested delivery date (subject to the applicable Product Lead Time); (e) any shipping instructions, including preferred carrier and shipping destination; and (f) the specifications for the Product. Notwithstanding anything contained in this Agreement and the China JV Operating Agreement to the contrary, and for the sake of clarity, [***].
perturbation.justification: The original text states the price is in Schedule 2. The changed text says the price is at the Supplier's discretion irrespective of Schedule 2. This directly contradicts section 2.2, which states the Purchase Order must include 'the price of each Product ordered per Schedule 2 attached hereto'. This creates a direct conflict, making it unclear how the price is determined, thus indicating a strong in-text contradiction and a structural flaw.
